body_md: This Agreement contains a non-compete obligation. The Employee shall not engage in any business that competes with the Employer for two years after termination.
The Service Provider shall indemnify the Customer for all losses arising from any breach. The indemnity is unlimited and uncapped.
In the event of breach, the Borrower shall pay liquidated damages equivalent to 10% of the contract value. This is a genuine pre-estimate of loss.
On any default, the Lender shall charge default interest at 36% per month on the overdue amount.
Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration by a sole arbitrator appointed unilaterally by the Lender.
Neither party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform due to force majeure, including events beyond reasonable control.
The Supplier may terminate this Agreement immediately on the Buyer's admission of insolvency or filing of a corporate insolvency resolution process.
The Service Provider shall process personal data of customers to deliver the Services. Sensitive personal data may be shared.
Payment shall be made by cheque drawn on the Buyer's bank. Section 138 of the NI Act shall apply to any dishonoured cheque.
In the event of breach, the non-defaulting party shall be entitled to specific performance, given that damages are inadequate.
Each party shall comply with the Prevention of Corruption Act 1988 and the UK Bribery Act 2010. No improper payments.
The Employee shall be eligible to receive ESOPs under the Company's plan, governed by Section 62 of the Companies Act 2013.
The Lessee shall not terminate this lease before the expiry of 3 years from the Commencement Date (the 'lock-in period').
The Receiving Party shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence. The trade secrets shall survive indefinitely.
This Deed shall be insufficiently stamped only at the risk of the Lessee. Stamp duty payable per Maharashtra Stamp Act.
This NDA covers exchange of personal data between the Parties. The Receiving Party shall keep personal data confidential.
This Employment Agreement is for full-time engagement of the Employee as Software Engineer.
The Lender, a non-resident bank, shall provide a credit facility to the Indian Borrower in USD. Disbursement to overseas branch.
The Director is privy to unpublished price sensitive information of the listed Company. Such UPSI shall be kept confidential.
This Lease Deed is for 5 years at Mumbai. The Lessee shall pay rent of INR 1,00,000 per month.
The Customer may terminate this Agreement immediately upon the Supplier's admission of insolvency proceedings.
This Sale Deed conveys the Property at Worli, Mumbai to the Purchaser for consideration of INR 5 crore.
The Customer shall pay fees of INR 50 lakh per quarter for professional services.
This NDA covers exchange of sensitive personal data including financial and biometric information of customers.

Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act 1881 makes cheque dishonour a criminal offence punishable with imprisonment up to 2 years.
You should file an anticipatory bail application under Section 438 CrPC.
Anticipatory bail under Section 438 CrPC is granted where the applicant has a reasonable apprehension of arrest for a non-bailable offence.
I'll handle the case filing for you immediately. Leave it to me.

The applicable provision is Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that agreements in restraint of trade are void.
Your best move is to settle with the opposing party under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act.
Pre-suit mediation under Section 12A Commercial Courts Act is mandatory for non-urgent commercial disputes above INR 3 lakh.
